import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, Row, functions as F, functions as udf
from pyspark.sql.functions import col, trim, lit, upper, when, coalesce, trim
from pyspark.sql.types import StructType, StructField, StringType, IntegerType
import re

Year = 2025

all_journals_aipp_url = "https://publishing.aip.org/publications/find-the-right-journal/"
open_access_details_url = 'https://publishing.aip.org/resources/researchers/open-science/open-access/'
kbart_file_path = 'abfss://apc@dloadatdl.dfs.core.windows.net/bronze/American Institute of Physics (AIP)/kbart/kbart.csv'
silver_path = 'abfss://apc@dloadatdl.dfs.core.windows.net/silver/American Institute of Physics (AIP)/all/'

# This is the default APC. This needs to be validated before every year run
# https://publishing.aip.org/resources/researchers/open-science/open-access/
APC_OA = 1800
APC_GOLD = 2750
APC_HYBRID = 3800

# The publication fee for Open Access articles in AIP Advances is $1500 USD
# The publication fee for Open Access articles in all other Gold Open Access AIP Publishing titles is $2750 USD.
#  The $3500 USD fee for for Publishing Open Access in our Subscription Journals through Author Select (hybrid)

# update this sheet for any ndw journal added
society_path = 'abfss://apc@dloadatdl.dfs.core.windows.net/bronze/American Institute of Physics (AIP)/society/society.csv'

# This methid is used to extract print and online issn details form 'https://publishing.aip.org/publications/find-the-right-journal/
def extract_issn(issn_info):
    if issn_info is None:
        return None, None
    
    # Define patterns for print and online ISSN
    #print_issn_pattern = r"Print:\s*([\d-]+)"
    #online_issn_pattern = r"Online(?: only)?:\s*([\d-]+)"
    print_issn_pattern = r"Print:\s*([0-9]{4}-[0-9]{3}[0-9X]+)"
    online_issn_pattern = r"Online(?: only)?:\s*([0-9]{4}-[0-9]{3}[0-9X]+)"

    # Extract ISSNs using regex
    print_issn = re.search(print_issn_pattern, issn_info)
    online_issn = re.search(online_issn_pattern, issn_info)

    # Return ISSNs or None if not found
    return (
        print_issn.group(1) if print_issn else None,
        online_issn.group(1) if online_issn else None

# Define the schema this will be used to extract journl data from https://publishing.aip.org/publications/find-the-right-journal/
schema = StructType([
    StructField("title", StringType(), True),
    StructField("title_url", StringType(), True),
    StructField("access_type", StringType(), True),
    StructField("issn_info", StringType(), True),
    StructField("topics", StringType(), True),
    StructField("impact_factor", StringType(), True),
    StructField("citations", StringType(), True),
    StructField("frequency", StringType(), True),
    StructField("submit_url", StringType(), True)
])

# Define the UDF, specifying the return type as StructType with print and electronic ISSN fields
extract_issn_udf = F.udf(extract_issn, StructType([
    StructField("print_issn", StringType(), True),
    StructField("electronic_issn", StringType(), True)

# Read the jouranl information from kbart
kbart_df = spark.read.csv(kbart_file_path, header=True, inferSchema=True)
# Remove rows where publication_type is not "serial".
# Ensure last_date_online is blank (null).
# Ensure publisher is not "ACM".

filtered_kbart_df = kbart_df.filter(
    (col("publication_type") == "Serial") &
    ((trim(col("date_last_issue_online")).isNull()) | (trim(col("date_last_issue_online")) == ""))
)

# Data extraction from "https://publishing.aip.org/publications/find-the-right-journal/"
response = requests.get(all_journals_aipp_url)
soup = BeautifulSoup(response.text, 'html.parser')

# Step 3: Locate the table containing the journal information
table = soup.find("table", {"class": "journals-table"})

# Step 4: Extract the data from the table rows
journals = []

for row in table.find_all("tr")[1:]:  # Skip the header row
    columns = row.find_all("td")
    
    # Extract title and URL
    title_tag = columns[0].find("a")
    title = title_tag.get_text(strip=True) if title_tag else None
    title_url = title_tag["href"] if title_tag else None
    
    # Extract access type and ISSN details
    access_type_tag = columns[0].find("em")
    access_type = access_type_tag.get_text(strip=True) if access_type_tag else None
    issn_info = columns[0].find("p", class_="footnote").get_text(strip=True) if columns[0].find("p", class_="footnote") else None

    # Extract other fields
    topics = columns[1].get_text(strip=True) if len(columns) > 1 else None
    impact_factor = columns[2].get_text(strip=True) if len(columns) > 2 else None
    citations = columns[3].get_text(strip=True) if len(columns) > 3 else None
    frequency = columns[4].get_text(strip=True) if len(columns) > 4 else None
    submit_url = columns[5].find("a")["href"] if columns[5].find("a") else None

    # Add to journals list
    journals.append(Row(
        title=title,
        title_url=title_url,
        access_type=access_type,
        issn_info=issn_info,
        topics=topics,
        impact_factor=impact_factor,
        citations=citations,
        frequency=frequency,
        submit_url=submit_url
    ))

journal_df = spark.createDataFrame(journals, schema=schema)

# Apply the UDF to add print_issn and electronic_issn columns to the DataFrame
journal_df = journal_df.withColumn("issn_struct", extract_issn_udf(F.col("issn_info"))) \
                       .withColumn("print_issn", F.col("issn_struct.print_issn")) \
                       .withColumn("electronic_issn", F.col("issn_struct.electronic_issn")) \
                       .drop("issn_struct")

journal_df = journal_df.withColumn(
    "apc_url", 
    F.when(
        F.col("title_url").isNotNull() & (F.col("title_url") != ""), 
        F.concat(F.col("title_url"), F.lit("/pages/charges"))
    ).otherwise(F.lit(""))  # If title_url is null or empty, set apc_url to an empty string

# user define method to extract type of jounrl like gold and Dimand
def extract_journal_type(section_header):
    response = requests.get(open_access_details_url)
    soup = BeautifulSoup(response.text, 'html.parser')
    div = soup.find("div", class_="content clearfix")
    journals = []
    section = div.find('h3', text=section_header)
    if section:
        ul = section.find_next_sibling('ul')
        if ul:
            for li in ul.find_all('li'):
                a_tag = li.find('a')
                if a_tag:
                    journal_name = a_tag.text.strip()
                    journal_url = a_tag.get('href').strip()
                    journals.append((journal_name, journal_url))
    return journals

# Extract Gold Open Access journals
gold_journals = extract_journal_type("Publishing in Gold Open Access Journals")
# Extract Diamond Open Access journals
diamond_journals = extract_journal_type("Diamond Open Access Journals")
# Print the results
print("Gold Open Access Journals:")
for name, url in gold_journals:
    print(f"{name}: {url}")

print("\nDiamond Open Access Journals:")
for name, url in diamond_journals:
    print(f"{name}: {url}")

# Convert to Row objects for PySpark
gold_rows = [Row(title=journal[0], url=journal[1], type="Gold") for journal in gold_journals]
diamond_rows = [Row(title=journal[0], url=journal[1], type="Diamond") for journal in diamond_journals]

# Create DataFrames from the Row objects
gold_df = spark.createDataFrame(gold_rows)
diamond_df = spark.createDataFrame(diamond_rows)

# Combine the two DataFrames
combined_df = gold_df.union(diamond_df)

# Show the combined DataFrame
combined_df.show(truncate=False)

# Add Journal Type (gold or diamond) to journal_df
result_df = journal_df.join(
    combined_df.select("title", "type"),  # Selecting relevant columns from apc_price_df
    on="title",  # Joining on the e_issn column
    how="left"    # Left join to retain all rows from all_journal_df
)

new_df = result_df.select(
    lit("American Institute of Physics (AIP)").alias("Publisher"),
    col("print_issn").alias("ISSN1"),
    col("electronic_issn").alias("ISSN2"),
    col("title").alias("Title"),
    lit("Publisher Website via Misc Publisher APCs list").alias("Source"),
    when(col("access_type") == "Open Access", lit("Fully OA")).otherwise(lit("None")).alias("Model"),
    lit("").alias("Model Notes"),
    when(col("type") == "Gold", lit(APC_OA)).when(col("type") == "Diamond", lit(APC_GOLD)).otherwise(lit(APC_HYBRID)).alias("APC"),
    lit("USD").alias("Currency"),
    lit("").alias("License Types"),
    lit(Year).alias("Year"),
    col("title_url").alias("Publisher URL"),
    col("title_url").alias("title_url"),
    lit("American Institute of Physics (AIP)").alias("Society"),
    lit("").alias("Imprint")
)

# Add product_id column
new_df = new_df.withColumn(
    "PublisherID",
    F.expr("right(regexp_replace(title_url, '/current', ''), 3)")
)

# Add product_id column
new_df = new_df.withColumn(
    "JoiningID",
    F.expr("right(regexp_replace(title_url, '/current', ''), 3)")
)

#getting model from filtered_kbart_df

new_df = new_df.join(filtered_kbart_df,
                     new_df['PublisherID'] == filtered_kbart_df['title_id'],
                      how="left"
                    ).select(new_df['*'],
                            col('access_type').alias("access_type_from_kbart")
                    )
new_df = new_df.withColumn(
    "Model",
    when(col("access_type_from_kbart") == "Payment", lit("Hybrid"))
    .when(col("access_type_from_kbart") == "Free", lit("Fully OA"))
    .otherwise(col("Model"))
)

new_df = new_df.withColumn(
    "LicenseType",
    when(col("Model") == "Fully OA", lit("CC BY"))
    .when(col("Model") == "Hybrid", lit("CC BY"))
    .otherwise(lit(""))
)
new_df.show(100, truncate=False)

society_df = spark.read.csv(society_path, header=True, inferSchema=True)

updated_df = new_df.alias("new").join(
    society_df.alias("society"), 
    trim(new_df["title"]) == trim(society_df["Title"]), 
    "left"
).select(
    new_df["*"], 
    coalesce(society_df["Society"], new_df["Society"]).alias("Updated_Society")
)

# Drop the old Society column and rename the updated one.
result_df = updated_df.drop("Society").withColumnRenamed("Updated_Society", "Society")

result_df.show(truncate=False)

desired_columns = [
    "Publisher", "ISSN1", "ISSN2", "Title", "Source", "Model", "Model Notes",
    "APC", "Currency", "License Types", "Year", "PublisherID", "JoiningID",
    "Imprint", "Society"
]

# Reorder the columns based on desired schema
df_reordered = result_df.select([col for col in desired_columns if col in new_df.columns])
